sections.name: People
sections.description: Capture the people signal that tells the AM who can sponsor change, who owns execution, & where expansion may stall.

items.question: Who are the key leaders across executive, clinical, operations, finance, & IT?
items.why: Shows who matters in evaluation, adoption, & expansion.

items.question: Who is the executive sponsor for change or growth?
items.why: No sponsor usually means no momentum for expansion.
items.risk: No sponsor means no internal momentum.

items.question: Who owns rollout after purchase?
items.why: Execution ownership determines whether adoption can stick.
items.risk: Adoption risk stays high even if the sale closes.

items.question: Which functions are centralized versus local?
items.why: Centralization shows where one motion can scale across the group.
items.risk: The team may pitch the wrong level of solution.

items.question: Who outside the organization influences strategic direction?
items.why: Hidden influencers often shape priorities, timing, & objections.
items.risk: Hidden decision pressure may delay or derail the motion.

sections.name: Process
sections.description: Capture the process signal that shows where work is manual, fragmented, inconsistent, or dependent on third parties.

items.question: Do they standardize workflows across locations?
items.why: Standardization signals whether change can scale cleanly.
items.risk: Rollout effort & adoption risk are harder to predict.

items.question: Which workflows vary most by office?
items.why: Variation often creates friction, inefficiency, & white space.

items.question: How is patient communication managed today?
items.why: Shows whether engagement is fragmented, manual, or vendor dependent.
items.risk: Engagement gaps & vendor overlap may be missed.

items.question: How is insurance handled today?
items.why: Insurance workflow maturity affects operational fit & service needs.
items.risk: RCM related pain points may be misunderstood.

items.question: How are payments handled today?
items.why: Payment friction often reveals automation or platform opportunity.
items.risk: Automation or platform fit may be mis-scoped.

sections.name: Technology
sections.description: Capture the technology signal that shows what systems are in place, what is fragmented, what vendors are active, & where reporting or adoption gaps exist.

items.question: What products do they already own from us?
items.why: Shows current footprint, adoption depth, & adjacent expansion paths.
items.risk: The team may miss adoption & expansion opportunities.

items.question: What PMS systems are in use today?
items.why: Mixed systems usually create complexity & opportunity.

items.question: What imaging platforms are in use today?
items.why: Imaging diversity may affect integrations, workflow, & standardization.
items.risk: Integration or workflow needs may be missed.

items.question: Which third party vendors are active, & what job does each do?
items.why: Vendor sprawl is often the clearest white space map.

items.question: How are KPIs & reporting handled today?
items.why: Manual reporting is a strong signal for analytics or automation opportunity.
items.risk: Analytics & visibility gaps may remain invisible.
